title: ranunculus dissectus subsp. huetii
intro: Ranunculus dissectus subsp. huetii, also known as the Huet's Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and finely dissected leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Huet's Buttercup include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, due to its active compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
